affected_files.purpose: EdgeLog: manager of per-source-fileID append-only edge logs, each backed by its own engine/wal.Writer instance, so concurrent appends to different fileIDs never contend on a shared lock.

TestPerNodeEdgeLog (-race): concurrent appenders across many distinct fileIDs; correctness + non-blocking checks. Plus supporting unit tests (open/close, invalid type rejection, multi-segment read-back).
Document 3.1.2's edgelog.go design under the existing 'per-node edge log' bullet, mirroring 3.1.1's CSR subsection style.

modules_not_touched: engine/graph/edge_append.go (EdgeAppender, task-2b.3.4) - deliberately left untouched, distinct narrower mechanism for split edges; scope decision documented in architecture-discovery.md
engine/graph/csr.go (task-3.1.1) - read-only reuse of CSREdge type, no modification
engine/split/execute.go - no change; still uses EdgeAppender, not affected by this subtask
dependents: 3.1.3 (compaction, future subtask) will read EdgeLog contents and merge into CSR array
engine/agents/ingestion (future, not yet built) will be the primary writer once implemented

reused_types: graph.CSREdge (from csr.go, unmodified) - reused verbatim as the per-node log entry shape
engine/wal.Writer / wal.OpenWriter / wal.ReadSegment (unmodified) - same low-level segment-writer primitive edge_append.go already uses

risk_notes: New, additive package-internal code; no existing exported API changed.

Directory-per-fileID layout means large numbers of distinct fileIDs create many small directories/files - acceptable for this subtask's scope (compaction, 3.1.3, is the mechanism that bounds long-term growth), flagged as a forward-looking note only, not a blocking concern here.
